visa_types.documents_required:
Travel medical insurance is mandatory for all Schengen countries. If you have not purchased,Click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/kwt/en/grc/travel-insurance)
Visa Requirements – Please click here (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/3Fkuaf9rLCJgcrqh0LZb53/401875cde4c12ee78c8b45cea3e1ca41/EEA-EU-Checklist-Latest.pdf) 
For applicants below 18 years of age, a duly filled/signed “Minor’s Travel Permission” is required. Please click here (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/23IwCnMMhTAFLgS4vetKFx/87631f4755e8c006f510c00e93705bea/Minors_travel_permission.pdf)
Please Note that both the Parents' Passport copies (even signature page), Civil ID copy and Residence Permit Copies(for non-Kuwaiti) to be attached with the application.
                                                                        If the parent(s) would be travelling along with kid(s), the valid visa copy should be attached (if any).
It is compulsory for  both the parents to visit the Visa Application Centre in order to sign the  Minor’s Travel Permission form at the submission counter itself [for the kids  travelling with or without parent(s)].click here (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/23IwCnMMhTAFLgS4vetKFx/87631f4755e8c006f510c00e93705bea/Minors_travel_permission.pdf)
INFORMATION 
For Persons applying for EEA/EU Spouse Visa (stay not more than 90 days)DOCUMENTS REQUIRED

- Original passport and    copy of previous Schengen visas.
For new passports, the old passport is also required. Passport should be
valid for at least six (6) months
from the date of visa issuance.
- Two colored    passport-size photos
of good quality (white background)in compliance with ICAO standards.
- Visa application form completed in English and signed
(name should be written as in passport).
- Copy of Kuwait Residence Permit (for Non-Kuwaiti) / civil ID copy (for GCC citizens)
Kuwait    residency should be valid for at least six (6) months
(for non-Kuwaiti
                                                                            and GCC citizens).
- International medical    travel insurance
for the duration of the trip covered not less than 30,000 Euros.
- Copy of return airline/flight ticket and confirmed hotel voucher.
Note: If travelling to other Schengen areas/countries, flight and hotel vouchers for all destinations must be
                                                                            submitted. Applicant may be asked to submit the proof of payment.
- Original and 1 copy of the marriage certificate The certificates provided must be issued from Greece
(in case of Greek citizen’s family members) OR from the relevant
                                                                            EEA/EU member state (in case of EEA/EU citizen’s family member). In the last case, if they are not in English, they must be translated in English with a copy of the original document
                                                                            attached.
- Original or certified copy of passport of the EU/EAA citizen .
- Support letter from the EU/EAA citizen .

visa_types.overview:
Overview
Once received by the Visa Application Centre, applications will be sent to the Embassy of Hellenic Republic in Kuwait the next working day & the applicant will be given an appointment
                                                                        for a personal Interview (if needed) with the Embassy of Hellenic Republic in Kuwait. Once the assessment is completed by the Embassy, the decision together with the passport &
                                                                        the other relevant documents will be available for collection from the Visa Application Centre.  Visa is valid to visit all the Schengen States. Only Kuwait nationals and nationals of third countries that are legal residents in Kuwait can apply for a visa at the Greece Visa
                                                                        Application Centre in Kuwait. Visa applicants are requested to appear in person at the time of submission.  Incomplete, unclear or unsigned applications will not be accepted. Visa fees are non-refundable and have to be paid only in cash at the time of application If the visit involves two or more Schengen countries, the Embassy responsible for the issuance of the visa will be: 
- The Embassy of the country in which the traveler is going to stay the longest.
- If the traveler is to stay the same time in different Schengen countries, the Embassy of the country of first entry.
Applicant’s lack of Travel plan will not fall on Embassy priority to process your visa. It is advisable to plan your travel well in advance to avoid any inconvenience
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
Once received by the Visa Application Centre, applications will be sent to the Embassy of Hellenic Republic in Kuwait the next working day & the applicant will be given an appointment
                                                                        for a personal Interview (if needed) with the Embassy of Hellenic Republic in Kuwait. Once the assessment is completed by the Embassy, the decision together with the passport &
                                                                        the other relevant documents will be available for collection from the Visa Application Centre.  Visa is valid to visit all the Schengen States. Only Kuwait nationals and nationals of third countries that are legal residents in Kuwait can apply for a visa at the Greece Visa
                                                                        Application Centre in Kuwait. Visa applicants are requested to appear in person at the time of submission.  Incomplete, unclear or unsigned applications will not be accepted. Visa fees are non-refundable and have to be paid only in cash at the time of application If the visit involves two or more Schengen countries, the Embassy responsible for the issuance of the visa will be: 
- The Embassy of the country in which the traveler is going to stay the longest.
- If the traveler is to stay the same time in different Schengen countries, the Embassy of the country of first entry.
Applicant’s lack of Travel plan will not fall on Embassy priority to process your visa. It is advisable to plan your travel well in advance to avoid any inconvenience

## Visa Fees

[grc > kwt > en > Table:EEA/ EU Spouse > Visa Fees 1]
All payments will be collected in local currency (cash only) as per the local exchange rate. 
- The applicable visa fee in Euro is as per the current exchange rate. It is subject to change without notice.
- All fees are non-refundable

## Documents Required
Travel medical insurance is mandatory for all Schengen countries. If you have not purchased,Click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/kwt/en/grc/travel-insurance)
Visa Requirements – Please click here (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/3Fkuaf9rLCJgcrqh0LZb53/401875cde4c12ee78c8b45cea3e1ca41/EEA-EU-Checklist-Latest.pdf) 
For applicants below 18 years of age, a duly filled/signed “Minor’s Travel Permission” is required. Please click here (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/23IwCnMMhTAFLgS4vetKFx/87631f4755e8c006f510c00e93705bea/Minors_travel_permission.pdf)
Please Note that both the Parents' Passport copies (even signature page), Civil ID copy and Residence Permit Copies(for non-Kuwaiti) to be attached with the application.
                                                                        If the parent(s) would be travelling along with kid(s), the valid visa copy should be attached (if any).
It is compulsory for  both the parents to visit the Visa Application Centre in order to sign the  Minor’s Travel Permission form at the submission counter itself [for the kids  travelling with or without parent(s)].click here (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/23IwCnMMhTAFLgS4vetKFx/87631f4755e8c006f510c00e93705bea/Minors_travel_permission.pdf)
INFORMATION 
For Persons applying for EEA/EU Spouse Visa (stay not more than 90 days)DOCUMENTS REQUIRED

- Original passport and    copy of previous Schengen visas.
For new passports, the old passport is also required. Passport should be
valid for at least six (6) months
from the date of visa issuance.
- Two colored    passport-size photos
of good quality (white background)in compliance with ICAO standards.
- Visa application form completed in English and signed
(name should be written as in passport).
- Copy of Kuwait Residence Permit (for Non-Kuwaiti) / civil ID copy (for GCC citizens)
Kuwait    residency should be valid for at least six (6) months
(for non-Kuwaiti
                                                                            and GCC citizens).
- International medical    travel insurance
for the duration of the trip covered not less than 30,000 Euros.
- Copy of return airline/flight ticket and confirmed hotel voucher.
Note: If travelling to other Schengen areas/countries, flight and hotel vouchers for all destinations must be
                                                                            submitted. Applicant may be asked to submit the proof of payment.
- Original and 1 copy of the marriage certificate The certificates provided must be issued from Greece
(in case of Greek citizen’s family members) OR from the relevant
                                                                            EEA/EU member state (in case of EEA/EU citizen’s family member). In the last case, if they are not in English, they must be translated in English with a copy of the original document
                                                                            attached.
- Original or certified copy of passport of the EU/EAA citizen .
- Support letter from the EU/EAA citizen .

## Photo Specifications
Applicants are requested to submit their application form with the new photo format, as explained below: 
General
The photo must fulfill the norm specified by the ICAO (International Civil Aviation Organization). 
- Use plain light-coloured background.
- The photo must be no more than 6-months old.
- Not tilted picture (portrait style) showing both edges of face clearly.
- Close up of the head and top of the shoulders so that the face takes up 70–80% of the photograph.
- Photo in sharp focus and clear.
Photo Size
The photo for each visa applicant must measure: 
- The picture dimensions must be 240 pixels wide by 320 pixels high at least. A smaller picture will be rejected. For instance, a photo being 235 pixels by 320 pixels is not valid.
- On the other hand, the photo should keep a proportion of 3 x 4 (width x height) within limits. However, 250 x 340 pixels picture would be valid, but not a 290 x 340 pixels one.
- Finally, the image weight (size in Kbytes) can never be bigger than 120 Kbytes.
Resolution
- An image resolution is measured in DPI (dots per inch) and it's mainly used to control printing quality. Therefore, higher resolution means better printing quality.
- Photos resolution should be 300 dpi, so, when you scan the image, check this parameter carefully. However, bear in mind that the higher the resolution, the bigger the picture.
Resolution
- The only image format accepted is JPEG (Join Photograph Expert Grup Format). The other image formats such as BMP, TIFF, GIF, PNG, etc. will be rejected (a warning will be shown).
Attaching the Photo to the Application form
- Glue one photograph to the application form in the designated space.
Note : If children are traveling with their parents and are included inside the passport of the mother and/or the father, one photograph for each children MUST be glued to the application form of the father or the mother, depending on whom are they traveling with.
Please follow these instructions carefully. If the photograph presented do not meet these requirements your application will be considered incomplete. Please click here (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/23Klop1kGYtaKWa41ma9PC/f8260ed2f528653c830104b5a0af856e/annex_a_photograph_guidelines.pdf) for more information

## Download Forms
Please take a printout of the Visa application Form. You are required to fill in all details of the applicant in a legible handwriting and submit it along with the required documents
                                                                        to the Visa Application Center. 
Application for Schengen Visa (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/7zTnt7D6UiRBZAUzWuDY4z/394cc3af30555bb3c7ad41a5cf60a6d2/new-application-form.pdf)

visa_types.name: Transit Visa
visa_types.documents_required:
Travel medical insurance is mandatory for all Schengen countries. If you have not purchased,Click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/kwt/en/grc/travel-insurance)
Visa Requirements – Please click here  (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/7iv4zzUkScgCLb7S6Nwcr6/a39dd7d0af88f0e576e9bf36c3426ea2/Checklist-Transit-KUWAIT.pdf)
Airport visa allows you to wait for a connecting flight in the international area of the airport. This visa does not grant access to the Schengen area. Only some nationalities require
                                                                        an Airport Transit Visa Documents to be submitted with the visa application:
- A visa application form correctly completed and signed
- Two (02) recent color photographs (3.5 cm × 4 cm) in compliance with ICAO standards.
- Original Passport and copy of previous Schengen visas. The passport should be valid for at least six (6) months from the travel date and contain at least two (02) free pages.
- In case of a new passport, photocopies of the old passport are required
Copy of Kuwait Residence Permit (for Non-Kuwaiti) / Civil ID copy (for GCC citizens). Residence permit should be valid for at least 6 months. 
- International Travel Insurance
- A valid entry visa for the final country of destination
- A proof of transport arrangements (confirmed airplane ticket or bookings)

visa_types.name: Study - short course
visa_types.documents_required:
Travel medical insurance is mandatory for all Schengen countries. If you have not purchased,Click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/kwt/en/grc/travel-insurance)
DOCUMENTS REQUIRED
- Original and copy of the
passport main pages (front with personal info, residence, and Schengen visas)
. For new passports, the old passport is also required.
- Two colored passport-size photos
of good quality (white background).
- Visa application completed in English and signed
(name should be written as in passport).
- Passport should be
valid for at least six (6) months
from the date of visa issuance.
- Applicants should be
residents of Kuwait
and have a
valid residence of at least six (6) months
(for non-Kuwaiti and GCC citizens).
- Salary certificate
and authorized signature of his which registered in Chamber of Commerce
- Original & copy of the
work permit
from the Ministry of Labour of Kuwait (for non-Kuwaiti and GCC citizens).
- International medical travel insurance
for the duration of the trip covered not less than 30.000 Euros.
- Ticket and voucher of hotel reservation / alternate accommodation
- Proof of adequate financial means:
- Bank statement of the Parent.
- Proof of Bank account in Greece or proof of the credit card provided by the parent.
- Certificate of enrolment
or Acceptance/Admission letter from the University/College stating the following details:
- Duration of course
- Type of Course (Subjects undertaken)

- The picture dimensions must be 240 pixels wide by 320 pixels high at least. A smaller picture will be rejected. For instance, a photo being 235 pixels by 320 pixels is not valid.
- On the other hand, the photo should keep a proportion of 3 x 4 (width x height) within limits. However, 250 x 340 pixels picture would be valid, but not a 290 x 340 pixels one.
- Finally, the image weight (size in Kbytes) can never be bigger than 120 Kbytes.
Resolution
- An image resolution is measured in DPI (dots per inch) and it's mainly used to control printing quality. Therefore, higher resolution means better printing quality.
- Photos resolution should be 300 dpi, so, when you scan the image, check this parameter carefully. However, bear in mind that the higher the resolution, the bigger the picture.
Resolution
- The only image format accepted is JPEG (Join Photograph Expert Grup Format). The other image formats such as BMP, TIFF, GIF, PNG, etc. will be rejected (a warning will be shown).
Attaching the Photo to the Application form
- Glue one photograph to the application form in the designated space.
Note : If children are traveling with their parents and are included inside the passport of the mother and/or the father, one photograph for each children MUST be glued to the application form of the father or the mother, depending on whom are they traveling with.

Overview
Once received by the Visa Application Centre, applications will be sent to the Embassy of Hellenic Republic in Kuwait the next working day & the applicant will be given an appointment
                                                                        for a personal Interview (if needed) with the Embassy of Hellenic Republic in Kuwait. Once the assessment is completed by the Embassy, the decision together with the passport & the
                                                                        other relevant documents will be available for collection from the Visa Application Centre. 
Visa is valid to visit all the Schengen States. Only Kuwait nationals and nationals of third countries that are legal residents in Kuwait can apply for a visa at the Greece Visa Application
                                                                        Centre in Kuwait. Visa applicants are requested to appear in person at the time of submission. 
Incomplete, unclear or unsigned applications will not be accepted. Visa Fees are non-refundable and have to be paid only in cash at the time of application If the visit involves
                                                                        two or more Schengen countries, the Embassy responsible for the issuance of the visa will be: 
- The Embassy of the country in which the traveler is going to stay the longest.
- If the traveler is to stay the same time in different Schengen countries, the Embassy of the country of first entry.
Applicant’s lack of Travel plan will not fall on Embassy priority to process your visa. It is advisable to plan your travel well in advance to avoid any inconvenience

INFORMATION
This regime is applicable to spouses of EU-EEA citizens, minor descendants of either the EU-EEA citizen or the spouse, and major descendants or ascendants that depend economically from
                                                                        the EU-EEA relative
DOCUMENTS REQUIRED
- Original and copy of the
passport main pages (front with personal  info, residence, and Schengen visas).
For new passports, the old passport is also required.
- Two colored passport-size photos
of good quality (white background).
- Visa application completed in English and signed
(name should be written as in passport).
- Passport should be
valid for at least six (6) months
from the date of visa issuance.
- Applicants should be
residents of Kuwait
and have a
valid  residence of at least six (6) months
(for non-Kuwaiti and GCC citizens).
- Salary certificate
and authorized signature of his which registered in Chamber of Commerce
- Original & copy of the
work permit
from the Ministry of Labour of Kuwait (for non-Kuwaiti and GCC citizens).
- Bank statement
for the last six (6) months with present balance.
- International medical travel insurance
for the duration of the trip covered not less than 30.000 Euros.
- Ticket and voucher of hotel reservation
- Accreditation of the normative requirements on kinship (blood relationship), age, or where applicable, economic dependency:

We would advise you please check the list of requisite documents before submitting your application. This will minimize chances of rejection of your Visa Application due to incorrect
                                                                        or inadequate documents

## Photo Specifications
Applicants are requested to submit their application form with the new photo format, as explained below: 
General
The photo must fulfill the norm specified by the ICAO (International Civil Aviation Organization). 
- Use plain light-coloured background.
- The photo must be no more than 6-months old.
- Not tilted picture (portrait style) showing both edges of face clearly.
- Close up of the head and top of the shoulders so that the face takes up 70–80% of the photograph.
- Photo in sharp focus and clear.
Photo Size
The photo for each visa applicant must measure: 
- The picture dimensions must be 240 pixels wide by 320 pixels high at least. A smaller picture will be rejected. For instance, a photo being 235 pixels by 320 pixels is not valid.
- On the other hand, the photo should keep a proportion of 3 x 4 (width x height) within limits. However, 250 x 340 pixels picture would be valid, but not a 290 x 340 pixels one.
- Finally, the image weight (size in Kbytes) can never be bigger than 120 Kbytes.
Resolution
- An image resolution is measured in DPI (dots per inch) and it's mainly used to control printing quality. Therefore, higher resolution means better printing quality.
- Photos resolution should be 300 dpi, so, when you scan the image, check this parameter carefully. However, bear in mind that the higher the resolution, the bigger the picture.
Resolution
- The only image format accepted is JPEG (Join Photograph Expert Grup Format). The other image formats such as BMP, TIFF, GIF, PNG, etc. will be rejected (a warning will be shown).
Attaching the Photo to the Application form
- Glue one photograph to the application form in the designated space.

Visa Requirements – Please click here  (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/5bJ3pbA43qG2iutXf4uSAp/0426497bbc8a5f35788c1da710b4c809/Sports_Team_Docs_Required_180215.pdf)
INFORMATION
For Persons applying for Cultural / Sports events Visa (stay not more than 90 days)
 For applicants below 18 years of age, a duly filled/signed “Minor’s Travel Permission” is required. Please click here (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/23IwCnMMhTAFLgS4vetKFx/87631f4755e8c006f510c00e93705bea/Minors_travel_permission.pdf) 
Please Note that both the Parents' Passport copies (even signature page), Civil ID copy and Residence Permit Copies(for non-Kuwaiti) to be attached with the application.
 If the
                                                                        parent(s) would be travelling along with kid(s), the valid visa copy should be attached (if any). It is compulsory for both the parents to visit the Visa Application Centre in order to sign the Minor’s Travel Permission form at the submission counter itself [for the kids
                                                                        travelling with or without parent(s)].
                                                                    
DOCUMENTS REQUIRED
- Original and copy of the
passport main pages (front with  personal info, residence, and Schengen visas).
For new passports, the old passport is also required.
- Two colored passport-size photos
of good quality (white background).
- Visa application completed in English and signed
(name should be written as in passport).
- Passport should be
valid for at least six (6) months
from the date of visa issuance.
- Applicants should be
residents of Kuwait
and have a
valid residence of at least six (6)  months
(for non-Kuwaiti and GCC citizens).
- All  the team members should apply for the visa at the same time and same day,  altogether.
- Basic requirements – overall ticket, overall hotel (including the name), and travel & health insurance.
- Minor permission letter signed by both the parents (recommended that both the parents accompany the child at the time of submission of application to the vac), passport copies of
                                                                            both the parents attached with the birth certificate (English) copy, if any.
- In the minor permission letter, details of the travel companion – his/her name, passport no., relationship, passport copy and contact details – should be attached.
- Contact details and passport copies of all the persons who will be travelling with the team and would be responsible for the kids.
- A proper invitation letter from greece, stating the issuance date, details of the tournament, reason for their trip, the kids and staff names (advisable, with passport nos.), accommodation
                                                                            details stated clearly, the trip details for all the days to be spent in greece, organizer’s details (name, designation, contact numbers and email addresses).
- Rooming list of all the travellers.
- A letter from school – that the school is responsible for the expenditure and the travelers till they come back to kuwait along with a list of all the travelers with their passport
                                                                            no., nationality and age.
- In – charge of the trip (and his/her details) and whom they are talking with (his/her details) [on a separate page].
- Parent’s original salary certificate (English) – with residence permit copy, work permit copy, business permit copy, marriage certificate copy; and original bank statement (english)
                                                                            for 6 months - if a print out from the website than stamped by the respective bank – with a sufficient balance in the account.
(non - Kuwaiti)

- An image resolution is measured in DPI (dots per inch) and it's mainly used to control printing quality. Therefore, higher resolution means better printing quality.
- Photos resolution should be 300 dpi, so, when you scan the image, check this parameter carefully. However, bear in mind that the higher the resolution, the bigger the picture.
Resolution
- The only image format accepted is JPEG (Join Photograph Expert Grup Format). The other image formats such as BMP, TIFF, GIF, PNG, etc. will be rejected (a warning will be shown).
Attaching the Photo to the Application form
- Glue one photograph to the application form in the designated space.
Note : If children are traveling with their parents and are included inside the passport of the mother and/or the father, one photograph for each children MUST be glued to the application form of the father or the mother, depending on whom are they traveling with.

- The only image format accepted is JPEG (Join Photograph Expert Grup Format). The other image formats such as BMP, TIFF, GIF, PNG, etc. will be rejected (a warning will be shown).

visa_types.overview:
Overview
The Embassy of Greece in Kuwait is pleased to announce the opening of the Visa Application Centre, an external service provider collecting visa applications
Application for short-stay visas (Schengen visa) can be lodged at the external service provider that cooperates with the Embassy:
Address: Arraya Center, Tower 1, Floor 28, Al-Shuhada Street, Kuwait City (next to Courtyard Hotel by Marriott).
Once received by the Visa Application Centre, applications will be sent to the Embassy of Hellenic Republic in Kuwait the next working day & the applicant will be given an appointment for a personal Interview (if needed) with the Embassy of Hellenic Republic in Kuwait. Once the assessment is completed by the Embassy, the decision together with the passport & the other relevant documents will be available for collection from the Visa Application Centre.
Visa is valid to visit all the Schengen States. Only Kuwait nationals and nationals of third countries that are legal residents in Kuwait can apply for a visa at the Greece Visa Application Centre in Kuwait. Visa applicants are requested to appear in person at the time of submission.
Incomplete, unclear or unsigned applications will not be accepted. Visa Fees are non-refundable and have to be paid only in cash at the time of application If the visit involves
                                                                        two or more Schengen countries, the Embassy responsible for the issuance of the visa will be: 
- The Embassy of the country in which the traveler is going to stay the longest.
- If the traveler is to stay the same time in different Schengen countries, the Embassy of the country of first entry.
Applicant’s lack of Travel plan will not fall on Embassy priority to process your visa. It is advisable to plan your travel well in advance to avoid any inconvenience.
Time frame of the process:
- Applications shall be decided on within 15 calendar days of the date of the delivery to the Embassy of an application which is admissible and may be extended upto 30 calendar days
                                                                            or in exceptional cases upto 60 calendar days
- Applications shall be decided on within 10 calendar days of the date of the delivery to the Embassy of an application which is admissible for the citizens of the following countries
                                                                            due to Visa Facilitation Agreements:
- Armenia, Azerbaijan, FYROM, Georgia, Ukraine.
- Albania, Bosnia and Herzegovina, Moldova, Montenegro, Serbia (For holders of non-biometric passports only, since holders of biometric passports are exempted from visa requirement).
- Applications of EU/EEA family members shall be decided as soon as possible and no later than 15 calendar days of the date of the delivery to the Embassy of an application which is
                                                                            admissible.
Note: The applications are delivered to the Greece Embassy the next working day.

visa_types.overview:
All visa applicants for Greece are advised to schedule an appointment and visit the Joint Visa Application Centre in Kuwait to submit their applications, together with all necessary documents. Please
                                                            note that visas are issued only to residents of the Kuwait
It is advisable to prepare all your required documents corresponding to your travel dates, to avoid delay in the application process. 
Please note applications can only be submitted maximum 6 months prior to the intended travel dates.
Please note granting a visa is the prerogative of the Embassy for each application. Submission of all the documents does not imply “right” to be granted a visa. 
Please note that the Embassy of Greece, Kuwait City, Kuwait issues visas only for residents in its territory of operation - Kuwait.
